Are you a university student who wants to discover the world of TIM?

Find out how to write a thesis with us, get a university internship, or learn more about our masters and doctorates!

## Thesis

Would you like to develop a thesis with us?

Send your proposal to HRContact@telecomitalia.it with your CV attached.

Find out what areas are of greatest interest to TIM and present your thesis project proposal. If it fits with our company's areas of interest, we will invite you by email for a short interview.

If successful, you will be supported by a tutor who will follow the development of your work.

During your research, we will assess the thesis for possible publication and dissemination within TIM, with a view to Knowledge Sharing.

Thesis topics:

- Internet of Things
- Artificial Intelligence

- Cybersystems, Robotica

- Health & Wellbeing
- Big Data & Personal Data
- Smart Spaces & Mobile Interactions
- SDN Cloud Services
- User Experience
- Design Thinking
- Business Innovation

## Internships

We believe that it is crucial to bring the academic world closer to the business world. Because of this, we welcome interns interested in exploring specific topics through an internship at our offices, an opportunity to gain direct knowledge of working activity and practices.

Internships are for a minimum of 3 months to a maximum of 6 months and, if they are to be part of the intern's curriculum, an agreement must be made with the intern's university.

Check our [vacancies for internships]( that are in line with your profile and find out which universities are already affiliated with TIM.

**Masters and Doctorates**

Partnerships with research institutes and leading universities allow us to innovate our business model and processes. We are involved in specific masters and specialisation courses in areas and disciplines that we are seeking to develop.

Why do we do it? To…

- support innovative research projects with an "industry-oriented" approach
- train and attract young talent
- develop new skills for the business.

Leading Italian Telecom Innovates with AI

# TIM Extends Free Perplexity Pro Access: A Leap Forward in AI & Telecom

Last updated: 9/29/2025

TIM, Italy's top telecom operator, has extended its trailblazing offer of granting free one-year access to Perplexity Pro for its customers until mid-December 2025. This move fortifies TIM’s position as a leader in AI integration within telecom, allowing users to benefit from premium AI services typically costing €20 per month. By partnering with Perplexity AI, backed by heavy hitters like Jeff Bezos, TIM is demonstrating a commitment to digital innovation and customer-centric strategies in the evolving telecom landscape.

![Banner for TIM Extends Free Perplexity Pro Access: A Leap Forward in AI & Telecom](

## Introduction to TIM's Perplexity Pro Offer

TIM, Italy's foremost telecom operator, has announced an extension to their highly popular promotional package, offering customers a year of complimentary access to Perplexity Pro, an industry-leading AI assistant service. Initially set to expire sooner, the offer has now been extended until mid-December 2025, allowing more of TIM’s customer base to experience the benefits of cutting-edge AI technology without any added financial burden. This strategic move not only underscores TIM's commitment to digital innovation but also marks them as the first operator in Italy to integrate such an advanced AI tool into their service suite. By doing so, TIM aims to enhance its reputation as a trailblazer in artificial intelligence integration within the consumer telecommunications sector. More details about this offer can be found [here](

Perplexity Pro, the highlight of TIM's latest offer, is valued at approximately 20 euros per month. However, TIM customers have the opportunity to use this sophisticated AI platform at no cost for an entire year, which translates into significant savings of around 229 euros annually. This offer is a part of TIM’s broader strategy to not only increase customer satisfaction but to align with their Customer Platform and Open Innovation strategy, focusing on making daily digital interactions more accessible and streamlined. By offering a free trial of Perplexity Pro, TIM is providing its users with a chance to explore the extensive capabilities of AI in transforming everyday tasks. This initiative not only adds value to TIM's service portfolio but also places them at the forefront of AI adoption within the European telecom landscape. Further reading is available [here](

## Features and Benefits of Perplexity Pro

Perplexity Pro, a breakthrough AI platform, is reshaping the way users interact with technology by offering sophisticated features that enhance everyday digital tasks. One of the standout features of Perplexity Pro is its ability to provide real-time conversational answers supported by verified sources, ensuring accuracy and reliability. This is achieved through its integration with multiple large language models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini, which automatically select the best model for each query, optimizing response accuracy. Whether users need to generate content, summarize lengthy documents, or even create images, Perplexity Pro handles these tasks seamlessly and efficiently, making it a versatile tool for both personal and professional use.

The benefits of using Perplexity Pro extend beyond its robust functionalities; financially, users stand to gain considerably. The service, valued at around 20 euros per month, represents a significant investment in quality AI interaction. However, thanks to TIM's promotion, this exceptional service is being offered free for a year to its fixed and mobile consumer customers, delivering a collective value of approximately 229 euros annually. This move not only underscores TIM's commitment to pioneering digital innovation in the telecom sector but also highlights their strategic goal to integrate AI technologies into everyday life, making them more accessible and beneficial for all users.

Moreover, TIM's collaboration signifies a strategic alignment with its Customer Platform and Open Innovation strategy, aiming to streamline users' digital experiences through reliable AI. This initiative also places TIM at the forefront of AI integration in consumer telecom, setting a benchmark for its European counterparts. The offer complements TIM's broader strategy to not just meet but exceed consumer expectations by embedding cutting-edge AI tools into their digital ecosystem. These advancements are part of TIM's ongoing efforts to lead digital innovation within the industry, focusing on enhancing customer satisfaction and engagement.

The launch of Perplexity Pro by TIM also mirrors the growing industry enthusiasm and investment in AI, which is gaining traction among major telecom operators across Europe. Partnerships with influential players in the AI space, backed by high-profile investors like Jeff Bezos, Softbank, and Nvidia, amplify Perplexity AI's influence and fortify its position as a leader in AI-driven consumer services. This momentum is further bolstered by acquisition interest from tech giants like Apple, pointing to a promising future outlook for Perplexity AI and its role in shaping modern consumer experiences.

## Eligibility and Activation Process for TIM Customers

TIM offers an exciting promotion that makes sophisticated AI technology accessible to its customers. As part of this offer, TIM provides consumers a unique opportunity to activate and enjoy one year of free access to Perplexity Pro, an advanced AI platform. To tap into this benefit, TIM's fixed and mobile customers can activate the service through the TIM Party loyalty program. This program ensures that every TIM customer can easily obtain a free access code, usable on both desktop and smartphone devices. By making the activation process simple, TIM encourages widespread adoption and utilization of Perplexity Pro, reflecting its commitment to enhancing digital experiences for its users.

According to [Telecompaper]( TIM's strategic move to offer Perplexity Pro aligns with its broader customer platform and open innovation strategy. This initiative not only highlights TIM’s role as a pioneer in AI technology within the Italian telecom industry but also underscores its intent to simplify daily digital interactions for customers. Perplexity Pro, known for its capabilities in real-time conversational answers, content creation, and verified source integration, provides immense value to users accustomed to high-tech tools. TIM's initiative marks a significant step in making AI technology both accessible and beneficial for everyday consumers.

Unlocking Perplexity Pro’s features is as simple as logging into the TIM Party loyalty platform, facilitating a user-friendly experience that even the less tech-savvy can appreciate. Once logged in, users receive an access code to activate Perplexity Pro on their preferred device. This seamless integration of AI into consumer's lives stands as a testament to TIM's dedication to driving innovation and providing high-quality services to its customer base. By offering this AI service free for a year, valued at about 229 euros, TIM sets a new standard in customer service enhancements, digitally empowering its users and fortifying its market position in Italy.

## Value Proposition and Cost Analysis

The value proposition of TIM's one-year promotional offer of Perplexity Pro access is multifaceted, both enhancing the customer experience and demonstrating TIM's commitment to innovation. By providing free access to an AI service valued at approximately 20 euros per month, TIM is offering substantial monetary savings and added value to its consumers. This move also underscores TIM's strategic goal of integrating advanced AI technologies into its offerings, thus setting it apart from competitors. According to [this article]( TIM aims to lead the market by embedding AI into daily telecom usage, thereby enriching customer interactions and positioning itself as a forward-thinking digital service provider.

The cost analysis of this initiative reveals a strategic investment in customer retention and acquisition. Offering a service that would normally cost 229 euros yearly demonstrates TIM's willingness to absorb initial costs to foster long-term consumer engagement and loyalty. Such a gambit is integral to TIM's overall strategy of bolstering its digital service portfolio by locking in customer interest early, as highlighted in [First Online's report]( Additionally, by leveraging the TIM Party loyalty program, the company simplifies the activation process and ensures widespread usage, which can potentially convert free users into paying customers after the promotional period ends.

Moreover, these efforts provide a competitive edge in the telecom market, not just in Italy, but across Europe, where similar initiatives by telecom giants such as Telefonica and Deutsche Telekom are emerging. TIM's approach is particularly significant because it positions the company as the first in Italy to undertake such a comprehensive AI integration strategy. The initiative is a part of the broader trend where telecom companies transition from traditional service providers to comprehensive digital assistance platforms, highlighted in discussions on [VoIP Review]( This paradigm shift not only enhances TIM's brand value but also sets a new benchmark in the competitive landscape of digital communications.

In addition to strengthening its market position, TIM's promotion of Perplexity Pro plays a critical role in driving digital literacy and usage among its consumer base. By democratizing access to sophisticated AI tools, TIM ensures that all levels of users, from tech-savvy individuals to novices, can benefit from AI-enhanced interactions. As articulated in [Mobile World Live]( this engagement strategy will likely stimulate consumer interest in AI technologies and embed AI into everyday life, thus fostering a more technologically engaged and literate population. As users experience the practical benefits of the AI assistant, their reliance on such technologies for efficiency and productivity may significantly increase during and beyond the promotional period.

Therefore, the value proposition and cost-benefit analysis of TIM’s offer is not only about immediate monetary saving but also about positioning TIM as a leader in integrating cutting-edge AI into customer offerings. This strategy reflects TIM's vision of transforming the AI adoption landscape in consumer telecom, providing a blueprint for how digital providers can leverage technology to enhance customer value and achieve a unique market position. These initiatives are poised to redefine user expectations in the telecom sector, as noted in [Telecoms.com's report]( marking a new era of AI-centric customer service offerings.

## Strategic Goals Behind TIM's Offer

TIM’s decision to extend the offer of free access to Perplexity Pro until mid-December is strategically aimed at strengthening its position as a leader in digital innovation within Italy's telecommunications sector. By providing its customers with access to an advanced AI platform valued at around €229 annually, TIM is not only enhancing customer satisfaction but also cultivating a deeper integration of AI within everyday digital interactions. This aligns with its broader strategic goal of leveraging AI technology to enrich its service portfolio and customer experience, thereby setting a competitive benchmark in the industry. According to [Telecompaper]( this move also underscores TIM's commitment to simplifying user experience through cutting-edge technology and enhancing digital literacy among its customer base.

Moreover, TIM's offer can also be seen as a strategic move to foster strong customer loyalty and retention. By embedding Perplexity Pro into its customer loyalty program, TIM encourages frequent engagement and creates added value for its services, potentially reducing churn rates. This also ties into TIM's Open Innovation strategy, as it opens avenues for further exploration into AI-driven solutions, ensuring that the company remains at the forefront of technological advancements. Integrating Perplexity Pro serves as a demonstration of TIM's agility and willingness to adapt to new technologies to better serve its customer base, reinforcing its strategic goals of innovation and leadership in the telecom industry.

The promotional strategy behind TIM’s partnership with Perplexity AI is driven by a desire to lead the Italian market in AI-enhanced telecom services, a sector that is rapidly growing in importance. By being the first operator in Italy to offer such a service, TIM is not only enhancing its brand perception but is also actively participating in the evolving landscape of AI in telecommunications, which is becoming increasingly pivotal in Europe. This strategic move is likely to inspire similar initiatives among other operators, fostering a more robust digital ecosystem within Europe. The strong backing of investors and the potential acquisition interest from major companies highlight the growing significance of Perplexity AI as a transformative force in the market.

## Significance of Perplexity AI in the Telecom Industry

Perplexity AI has taken a significant step forward in shaping the future of the telecom industry through its collaboration with TIM, Italy's leading telecom provider. By offering a year of free access to Perplexity Pro, TIM has brought 1 [Perplexity AI's advanced capabilities]( into the hands of their customers. This move not only enriches TIM's digital offerings but also positions the company as a frontrunner in AI integration within telecommunications.

## Current Promotions and Competitive Landscape

TIM, Italy's leading telecom provider, has recently extended its groundbreaking promotion, offering one year of complimentary access to Perplexity Pro, an advanced AI platform, as detailed in [this report]( This initiative not only highlights TIM's commitment to integrating cutting-edge AI technology into its services but also strengthens its position within the competitive telecom market. The value of this promotion lies in its potential to significantly reduce costs for consumers, granting them access to a service typically priced at 20 euros per month. As the first in Italy to offer such a promotion, TIM sets a precedent in leveraging AI to enhance customer engagement and satisfaction.

The competitive landscape of the European telecom sector is witnessing a dynamic shift as leading operators like Telefonica and Deutsche Telekom follow TIM's footsteps by incorporating similar AI-driven promotions. This growing trend signifies an industry-wide move towards embracing advanced AI tools to enrich customer experiences. In doing so, these telecom giants aim to differentiate their offerings, fostering a competitive edge that extends beyond traditional connectivity services. As noted in [various analyses]( the integration of AI platforms like Perplexity Pro marks a pivotal shift towards more personalized and intelligent telecom services, a transition that continues to redefine the sector's competitive environment.

In the realm of telecom promotions, innovations such as TIM's Perplexity Pro offer not only position telecom operators as digital pioneers but also engender deeper customer loyalty by providing tangible value additions. The strategic launch of this promotion as part of TIM's broader digital innovation strategy underscores its ambition to be at the forefront of AI adoption in consumer services. Furthermore, as covered in [industry insights]( such initiatives are likely to incite competitive responses that could lead to a new era of AI-driven telecom service offerings across Europe.

According to [related reports]( this approach not only strengthens customer ties but also amplifies TIM's influence as an innovator in the telecom industry. Such advancements in consumer AI accessibility are poised to influence the broader telecom market, prompting a shift towards more inclusive and advanced consumer solutions.

## Public Reactions to the TIM Promotion

The recent extension of TIM's promotion offering a free one-year subscription to Perplexity Pro has generated a variety of public reactions. Many have taken to social media and forums to express their approval, acknowledging TIM's leadership role in leveraging advanced AI technology within Italy's telecommunications industry. The inclusion of this sophisticated AI assistant is seen as a progressive step towards enhancing digital accessibility for everyday users, enabling them to simplify tasks ranging from professional content generation to personal document summarization. According to [Telecom Paper]( this initiative marks TIM as a notable pioneer in AI integration.

Additionally, users have expressed significant interest in the operational capabilities that Perplexity Pro offers, such as real-time conversational responses and verified information sourcing. The broad functionality provides substantial appeal, with consumers eager to implement these features into their daily digital routines. However, discussions have also surfaced regarding the long-term sustainability of this offer. Some users are skeptical about what follows after the promotion ends, questioning whether the majority would be willing to transition to a paid model. As noted by [Telecom Paper]( this apprehension highlights concerns over customer loyalty and retention strategies.

On a broader scale, the reputation of Perplexity AI has been bolstered by its prestigious backing, catching the attention of tech influencers and industry observers. With notable investors like Jeff Bezos and companies such as Softbank keenly watching, the public view of Perplexity Pro transcends simply that of a telecom promotion; it is perceived as an indicator of shifting dynamics within the global AI landscape. This expansion into consumer services, also mirrored by offers from other European giants like Telefonica and Deutsche Telekom, underlines a growing trend noted by [Telecom Paper](

User feedback has largely praised the simplicity and efficiency of activating Perplexity Pro via TIM's loyalty program. Yet, while the activation process is deemed straightforward, there are calls for more robust user support to maximize engagement, particularly among those less familiar with AI technologies. Community insights suggest that increased tutorials and guides could enhance user satisfaction and confidence in using these advanced tools, as detailed by [Telecom Paper](

Moreover, alongside the overall positive reception, there are emerging discussions centered around privacy and data security concerns. While these discussions are less frequent, they highlight a critical dialogue about safeguarding user data amidst the growing integration of AI services. This caution, as highlighted by consumer feedback, reflects a balanced perspective where enthusiasm for innovation is tempered by the necessity for transparency in digital privacy practices. TIM's promotional strategy, thus, not only acts as a milestone in AI adoption but also as a potential leader in setting industry standards for consumer privacy, as discussed in [Telecom Paper](

## Economic, Social, and Political Implications

TIM's promotional offer of one year of free access to Perplexity Pro is likely to have several economic implications. By providing this advanced AI service at no additional cost, TIM could see heightened consumer loyalty and potentially attract new customers interested in cutting-edge technology. The integration of such premium services may encourage other telecom operators to follow suit, catalyzing industry-wide adoption of AI in telecommunication services. This move represents more than just an enhancement to TIM's digital offerings—it symbolizes a shift towards telecom companies becoming comprehensive digital service providers. The increas in technology-consumer synergy might lead to wider market expansion and demand for AI-driven solutions, paving the way for new business models centered around digital services, particularly in content creation and intelligent data analysis ( [source](

Socially, the extension of Perplexity Pro access democratizes AI technology across various demographics, potentially increasing digital literacy and productivity. This move could be particularly significant in Italy, where accessibility to advanced technology may vary. With tools provided by Perplexity Pro—such as real-time informational cross-referencing—users are empowered to make informed decisions, thus potentially reducing the influence of misinformation. As people incorporate AI more deeply into their daily lives, the need for educational initiatives around its ethical and responsible use becomes evident, ensuring that users are informed about data security and privacy issues. This societal shift towards routine AI usage might stimulate broader discussions on digital inclusivity and literacy in Italy and beyond ( [source](

Politically, TIM's strategic decision to offer Perplexity Pro could influence regulatory frameworks within Italy and Europe. By setting a precedent for AI integration in telecom services, TIM has positioned itself at the forefront of digital innovation, potentially affecting policy formulations regarding technology use and consumer rights within the context of AI. This initiative could also play a role in shaping Italy's position on digital advancements within the EU, promoting a competitive atmosphere that aligns with broader EU goals of technology sovereignty. Additionally, the involvement of high-profile investors and companies in this initiative underscores the geopolitical implications, highlighting the growing international competition to lead in the field of AI ( [source](

## Expert Predictions and Future Trends

As the telecommunications industry rapidly embraces artificial intelligence, experts predict significant shifts in consumer behavior and service delivery. With initiatives like TIM's free access to [Perplexity Pro]( consumers are expected to become more reliant on AI-driven solutions for day-to-day activities, from content creation to information gathering. This growing dependence highlights the critical role AI will play in redefining user expectations and generating new opportunities for innovative service offerings.

Future trends in AI integration suggest that telecommunications companies will increasingly focus on partnerships with AI-driven platforms, moving beyond traditional connectivity solutions. According to industry analysts, TIM's initiative to offer [one year of free access to Perplexity Pro]( marks a strategic pivot towards embedding AI as a core component of customer interactions. This trend could reshape competitive dynamics within the sector as operators vie to deliver value-added, AI-enhanced experiences.

The impact of advanced AI tools on the telecom sector extends beyond customer engagement to include operational efficiencies and revenue diversification. As more companies follow TIM's lead, offering similar promotions like [those initiated by Telefonica and Deutsche Telekom]( the market could witness increased collaborations between telecom providers and AI innovators. Such moves are anticipated to not only enhance service portfolios but also create new business models centered around AI capabilities.

Despite the promising outlook, challenges remain, particularly concerning user retention post-promotion. As highlighted by TIM's free year of [Perplexity Pro access]( telecom operators will need to strategize on monetization post-trial periods. Ensuring sustained user engagement and finding consumer-friendly pricing models will be crucial in securing long-term success in the AI-enhanced telecom sector.

# TIM Enterprise and Google Cloud boost cloud and AI ties

By [Simon Dux](

-

7 June 2024

0

[0](

Share

[Facebook]( "Facebook") [Twitter]( "Twitter") [Linkedin]( "Linkedin") [Pinterest]( "Pinterest") [WhatsApp]( "WhatsApp")

[More]( "More")

![](

## The two have launched a new technology hub, at the OGR Tech, is dedicated to developing and experimenting with the Cloud and AI

Long-term partners TIM and Google Cloud have built their first customer innovation centre at the OGR Tech – OGR Turin innovation hub that will be home to an intelligent laboratory dedicated to developing and experimenting with cloud technologies and AI to demonstrate the technologies’ potential when applied to different segments such as smart cities, tourism, manufacturing, retail and finance. It joins the existing centre, where TIM Enterprise and Google Cloud continue to operate at the Opening Future Hub in collaboration with Intesa Sanpaolo.

The move comes as Italy positions itself to lead the AI debate in Europe. Last month the government earmarked an initial investment of up to €1 bn to promote AI projects and startups, which will be backed by state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni recently said AI will be among the key issues of Italy’s presidency of the G7, which lasts until the end of this year.

This week Accenture published a study that concluded Italian companies could increase the added value they create by about €50 billion euros by 2030 with the generalised use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI), [according to Reuters](

**In for the long-haul**

The collaboration between TIM and Google Cloud dates back to 2020. TIM Group built new data centres to be the backbone infrastructure for two new Google Cloud regions in Italy. The Piedmontese capital is already home to one of the two Google Cloud regions, inaugurated in March last year. The Turin Region is housed in one of TIM Enterprise’s 16 data centres.

TIM’s cloud company Noovle is a key Google Cloud partner in Italy and was responsible for migrating just over a quarter (26%) of TIM’s private cloud solutions from on-premises data centers to Google Cloud, using Compute Engine to create and run virtual machines.

In 2020, TIM and Google Cloud launched of the first platform in Italy that enabled smart mobility on TIM’s Edge Cloud 5G technology. The platform combines the Google Distributed Cloud Virtual software with TIM’s Telco Cloud infrastructure and 5G mobile network. That project used TIM’s 5G network in the Bologna and Modena area to allow MASA (Modena Automotive Smart Area) and the University of Modena and Reggio Emilia test new solutions for self-driving and assisted cars, as well as applications for Advanced Cloud Mobility.

**TIM knows which way the wind is blowing**

Recent research carried out by the TIM Study Centre in collaboration with Intesa Sanpaolo Innovation Center shows that the AI market is developing strongly in Italy, as elsewhere, with an estimated value of €1.9bn in 2023, growing by 37% per year to around €6.6bn in 2027. This will be supported mainly by investments in the finance, ICT, TLC, manufacturing and retail sectors, with further growth potential in the healthcare, public administration and agriculture segments.

From 2022 to 2026, the cumulative contribution of AI to Italy’s GDP could reach up to €195bn, corresponding to an average annual value of almost 40 billion euros, or about 2% of GDP. The market is beginning to show increasing demand for automation and AI systems, and growth in that demand is estimated to increase by 52%, with both predictive analytics and production optimisation solutions accounting for a significant share: 60% of large companies across various sectors already use AI, according to Politecnico di Milano.

TIM Enterprise and Google Cloud intend to use the innovation centre to translate research into real use cases applicable to the market. There will be a focus on disruptive technologies such as Generative and Predictive Artificial Intelligence, Virtual and Augmented Reality, Machine Learning and the Internet of Things, offering smart solutions capable of developing the competitiveness and business of companies and public bodies against a constantly evolving backdrop.

“We want to simplify the technology choices of companies and public bodies that are now operating in an increasingly fast-paced market where the Cloud and AI are among the main accelerators of digital transformation,” said TIM chief enterprise and innovative solutions officer Elio Schiavo ( _above_). “We are leading players in these sectors – so much so that in 2023 we recorded cloud revenues of €1bn, with a strong growth trend that continued in the first quarter of this year.”

“The new Customer Innovation Center strengthens our relationship with companies, the public administration and our partners, such as Google Cloud, making us increasingly a standard bearer for innovation,” he added.

“Through collaboration and hands-on learning, the centre will equip companies and institutions with the tools to develop innovative solutions that address their specific challenges and place them at the heart of the digital transformation,” said Google Cloud customer engineering director, Italy, Paolo Spreafico.

The operator said demo areas are available in the centre to demonstrate the potential of the applications: these range from TIM Urban Genius, a platform for managing smart cities, and virtual and augmented reality solutions for the immersive enjoyment of content drawn from artistic and cultural attractions to services dedicated to enhancing entertainment, sustainable data centres and the various areas of use for generative and predictive AI.

# Telecom Italia jumps 4% as TIM Enterprise unveils €1 bln tech investment plan

Author [Navamya Acharya](

[Stock Markets](

Published10/03/2025, 06:52 AM

Telecom Italia jumps 4% as TIM Enterprise unveils €1 bln tech investment plan

[View all comments (0)0](

![© Reuters](

© Reuters

[TLIT\\
\\
4.11%](

Investing.com -- [Telecom Italia]( (BIT:TLIT) shares rose 4% on Friday after the group’s enterprise division announced a €1 billion investment plan aimed at accelerating Italy’s digital transformation.

The plan, presented to the financial community at the Santo Stefano Ticino Data Center, covers the period 2025 to 2027 and will focus on expanding cloud, edge computing, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, 5G and IoT services.

The program follows €350 million in investments made in 2024 and will bring the total to roughly €1 billion.

A major part of the spending will go toward the company’s data center network, which is expected to reach 125 MW of installed capacity across 17 sites, eight of them TIER IV or Rating IV certified.

The plan also calls for a new “AI-ready” facility and the upgrade of two existing data centers. Around €105 million will be dedicated to edge cloud expansion.

TIM Enterprise serves more than 30,000 business and public administration clients and reported revenues of €3.3 billion in 2024, with a gross margin of €1.2 billion and EBITDA After Lease of €700 million.

Cloud remains the largest source of revenue for the division, surpassing €1 billion last year, with further growth expected in 2025.

The investment plan is supported by a backlog of about €4 billion in contracts with leading companies and public administrations.

TIM Enterprise also plays a central role in the National Strategic Hub, a sovereign cloud initiative developed with Leonardo, CDP and Sogei.

4.11%](

Investing.com -- [Telecom Italia]( (BIT:TLIT) shares rose 4% on Friday after the group’s enterprise division announced a €1 billion investment plan aimed at accelerating Italy’s digital transformation.

The plan, presented to the financial community at the Santo Stefano Ticino Data Center, covers the period 2025 to 2027 and will focus on expanding cloud, edge computing, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, 5G and IoT services.

The program follows €350 million in investments made in 2024 and will bring the total to roughly €1 billion.

A major part of the spending will go toward the company’s data center network, which is expected to reach 125 MW of installed capacity across 17 sites, eight of them TIER IV or Rating IV certified.

The plan also calls for a new “AI-ready” facility and the upgrade of two existing data centers. Around €105 million will be dedicated to edge cloud expansion.

TIM Enterprise serves more than 30,000 business and public administration clients and reported revenues of €3.3 billion in 2024, with a gross margin of €1.2 billion and EBITDA After Lease of €700 million.

Cloud remains the largest source of revenue for the division, surpassing €1 billion last year, with further growth expected in 2025.

The investment plan is supported by a backlog of about €4 billion in contracts with leading companies and public administrations.

TIM Enterprise also plays a central role in the National Strategic Hub, a sovereign cloud initiative developed with Leonardo, CDP and Sogei.

# Telecom Italia's TIM to invest EUR1 billion in data centres in Italy

_translate_

![Flag](

13d

![User Avatar](

UCapital Media

Share:

![](

![](

![](

![article image](

Telecom Italia Spa on Thursday said subsidiary TIM Enterprise will commit EUR1 billion to Italy's "digital transformation" in the next three years.

The Rome-based telecommunications company aims for TIM Enterprise to "consolidate its role as the technological engine and point of reference for the TIM Group".

TIM Enterprises is positioning itself as an end-to-end digital services firm, covering cloud services, edge computing, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, 5G and 'internet of things'.

In addition, it is targeting an installed capacity of 125 megawatts for its data centre network. Eight of the 17 centres will be "Tier IV or Rating IV certified, representing the highest reliability standards", Telecom Italia said, accounting for half of all data centres in Italy with this certification.

TIM Enterprise plans to upgrade two centres, and establish what it described as an "AI-ready" facility.

Of the EUR1 billion set aside for investment plan between 2025 and 2027, EUR105 million will go towards the edge cloud division, "a key technology that enables low-latency, highly resilient digital services that are in increasingly high demand in the market," according to TIM.

TIM Enterprise invested EUR350 million in growth in 2024, and it has a EUR4 billion backlog, including corporate and public administration contracts, TIM said. TIM Enterprise's revenue totalled EUR3.3 billion in 2024, largely driven by its cloud offering, and has continued to grow in 2025, TIM added.

"We are developing a robust, secure, all-Italian infrastructure that can support ongoing technological transformation, and we are committed to building a truly independent and sustainable European ecosystem," said Elio Schiavo, TIM's chief enterprise & innovative solutions officer.

"This is not just a technological project; it is a strategy to ensure Italy's autonomy, resilience and competitiveness."

TIM Enterprise is part of the National Strategic Hub, a European cloud collaboration whose partners include Leonardo Spa.

![TIM](

TIM Group is stepping into the utility sector with the launch of TIM Energia, a new electricity service tailored for business owners, professionals, and small- to medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). This initiative aligns with TIM’s strategy to offer high-value services that support businesses with digital transformation and sustainable energy consumption.

Developed in partnership with Axpo Italia S.p.A., (Axpo) a subsidiary of the Swiss Axpo Group, TIM Energia brings over a century of expertise in [renewable energy solutions]( to the Italian market. Axpo operates in 40 countries and specializes in innovative and sustainable energy strategies.

**Latest:** [**TIM Finalizes Sparkle Sale to MEF and Retelit**](

### **A New Step in Customer-Centric Strategy**

TIM Energia is a key component of the TIM Group’s ‘Customer Platform’ strategy, which aims to provide advanced services beyond telecommunications, such as artificial intelligence ( [AI]( [cloud]( [cybersecurity]( and Internet of Things ( [IoT]( solutions. As part of this vision, TIM supports businesses with flexible and cost-effective renewable energy plans. Customers can choose between two options: the Flex Plan, which is based on market trends and offers pricing flexibility, and the Fix Plan, a 24-month fixed-price option ideal for those seeking stability and protection from price fluctuations. TIM Energia customers will receive an exclusive EUR 50 annual discount on their energy bills to celebrate the launch.

**Related:** [**Wired for Green: How Renewable Energy is Energizing Europe’s Telecom B2B Sector**](

Andrea Rossini, Chief Consumer, Small and Medium Business Market Officer, and Mobile Wholesale Market Officer at TIM, explained, “We want our customers to see us as a strategic partner, offering high-value services that support their businesses. This partnership with Axpo allows us to enter the energy market with solutions designed for SMEs, combining our best-in-class connectivity and technology with renewable energy services.”

Ivanhoe Romin, Axpo Italia’s General Manager, added, “Axpo Italia’s 25-year presence in Italy has been built on designing innovative solutions for our partners and customers. This collaboration with TIM marks a new milestone, reinforcing our role as market pioneers in providing flexible energy solutions that help Italian businesses navigate an increasingly volatile market while safeguarding their competitiveness.”

# With Azure AI, TIM is a pioneer in the implementation of a synthesized voice service to increase customer satisfaction and retention

[Share the story](

![](

TAIS, the company's virtual assistant, is supported by Azure and is able to meet the needs of TIM’s customers with the ability to change the speed and intonation of its voice, as well as speak in 140 different languages. Through its pioneering actions, TIM modernized its telephone answering service, reduced costs, and was able to get closer to its mission of becoming the mobile carrier most beloved by customers.

![TIM](

“I am extremely satisfied with the product. It exceeded my expectations. The technology is very advanced, and this constant evolution always opens up new possibilities and gives us a competitive edge.”

Olimpio Fernandes, Director of Cognitive Experience Center, TIM

How do you serve your customer over the phone with a satisfactory experience that solves almost any problem? This is one of the most challenging issues for companies in recent times; human customer service can be time-consuming, and a bot's script may not address certain issues. In the age of AI and a focus on personalized customer experiences, TIM, with support from Microsoft, found an effective and modern, agile response: a synthesized and realistic voice.

![](

### Looking for a friendly service

TIM is an Italian-Brazilian company founded in 1995, and present in Brazil since 1998. Today it serves tens of millions of customers daily throughout the nation. It is one of the largest in the country in landline, mobile telephone and internet services, and even offers Internet of Things (IoT), as well as digital security.

Customer service is performed by the company's virtual assistant, named TAIS. “We have a mantra, and we work so that our customers relate to TIM the same way they relate to their friends. Friendship is a choice, and while problems can happen, friendship remains," explains Olimpio Fernandes, director of the Cognitive Experience Center at TIM.

Initially, the telephone service was created with a robotic voice and some options to choose from, which was replaced by scripted human speech and recorded in a studio. "No one communicates with a friend through a list of options, and the voice recorded in the studio made it impossible for any type of improvisation and limited the scope of the conversation," Fernandes says.

TIM continued to search for a new customer service platform capable of assessing matters that were not previously planned and changing its speed according to the customer's context. Another issue was covering other languages to serve its foreign audience, given that it is an Italian-Brazilian company that usually sponsors major events.

![](

### A pioneering solution that cuts costs and generates more customer satisfaction and retention

With Microsoft's support, TIM implemented a synthesized and realistic voice solution for AI-generated phone answering. "We were pioneers here in Brazil. We made a courageous choice investing in the use of a neural, synthesized voice when no one else was doing it. We talk to millions of customers each year, so changing our voice is an act of courage,” says Olimpio.

The company has implemented [Azure Cognitive Services]( [Azure AI services]( as well as [Azure AI Speech]( and [Text-to-Speech Neural Voice]( which enable the neural voice channel for TIM.

"Our customers accepted the new speech with open arms, noticing the quality of understanding, intonation and transcription. The latency also worked without any complications," says Olimpio.

“With the switch to the neural voice, we were able to measure, via surveys, an increase in customer satisfaction by approximately 30 percent. Another important point is customer retention: services that no longer require human contribution have a direct influence on cost reduction and demand less effort from our customers. This improvement was very interesting," says Olimpio.

The concern about diversity and inclusion was also considered. In the tool, it is possible to request slower or faster speech, in addition to using more than 400 voices in 140 languages—expanding the service capacity for international customers, which is a common situation for an Italian-Brazilian company, such as TIM. "This process has brought us cost improvement with quality gains in service in a very broad way," Olimpio adds.

### An ever-evolving platform that allows you to achieve more

Olimpio celebrates the entire process that the company has gone through and says that the next step is to raise the potential of this experience. TIM aims to implement different tones in the voice for a more personalized and empathetic service, in addition to working even more on diversity and inclusion so that problems can be well communicated by customers and solved by the company.

“I am extremely satisfied with the product. It exceeded my expectations. The technology is very advanced, and this constant evolution always opens up new possibilities and gives us a competitive edge. I am very satisfied with the technology and support,” Olimpio concludes.

Find out more about [TIM]( on [Twitter]( [Facebook]( [LinkedIn]( and [YouTube](

“We were pioneers here in Brazil. We made a courageous choice of investing in the use of a neural, synthesized voice when no one else was doing it. We talk to millions of customers each year, so changing our voice is an act of courage.”

Olimpio Fernandes, Director of Cognitive Experience Center, TIM

# Telecom Italia's AI Tie-Up With Microsoft May Bring Job Cuts

Telecom Italia turns to Microsoft for AI expertise after previously announcing plans to cut 'human operated interactions' by 30%.

[![Picture of Iain Morris](

[Iain Morris]( International Editor, Light Reading

April 19, 2018

4 Min Read

![Light Reading logo in a gray background | Light Reading](

[Facebook](http://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=

Telecom Italia has served up troubling news for its customer services staff by announcing details of an artificial intelligence (AI) partnership with US software giant Microsoft.

The Italian phone incumbent today said it would use Microsoft's AI technology to develop chatbots and make other improvements to customer care and technical assistance.

A chatbot is a voice- or text-activated digital assistant that can be used instead of an employee to provide support to an organization's customers. According to Norwegian telecom incumbent Telenor, evidence suggests many customers would prefer to use a chatbot than speak to a customer services assistant. (See [Downsizing Telenor Pins Margin Hopes on Automation](

While operators like Vodafone Group plc (NYSE: VOD) have played down the jobs threat from chatbots, others have acknowledged the use of automation and digital tools will lead to headcount reductions. Telenor Group (Nasdaq: TELN) expects to cut one in five jobs in the next three years as it automates operations, while Russia's MTS, which has been piloting two chatbots, plans to cut 1,000 of 11,000 customer services jobs this year. (See [Russia's MTS to Cut 1,000 Jobs as AI, Chatbots Arrive](

Telecom Italia (TIM) representatives did not respond to queries about the likely impact of the Microsoft Corp. (Nasdaq: MSFT) partnership on staff numbers. But the operator is one of a number in Europe and North America that have already realized efficiency improvements through headcount reductions. (See [Efficiency Drive by Major Telcos Has Claimed 74K Jobs Since 2015](

The operator cut 1,800 jobs across its Italian and Brazilian units last year, or about 3% of the company total. Per-employee revenues rose 7.5% in 2017, to around $333,000.

Reporting its recent financial performance earlier this month, Telecom Italia said its digitization targets under a new "DigiTIM" plan are to get 85% of customers using self-care apps and reduce "human-operated interactions" by 30%. (See [Telecom Italia Molders as Shareholders Feud](

Should that ultimately translate to the disappearance of three in ten jobs at the customer services department, the impact will be heavy. While Telecom Italia does not break out details of staff numbers across different departments, about 15% of Mobile TeleSystems OJSC (MTS) (NYSE: MBT) employees were involved in customer services at the end of 2017. If the proportion were similar at Telecom Italia, around 2,670 jobs could be at risk, or about 4.5% of the company total.

The operator has indicated that its tie-up with Microsoft forms an important component of its DigiTIM plan.

The introduction of apps and "interactive voice response" technology would help to simplify its relationship with customers, said Telecom Italia.

For all the latest news from the wireless networking and services sector, check out our dedicated mobile content channel here on Light Reading.

While a number of US tech companies have been investing in AI-based digital assistants and chatbot technology, Microsoft and IBM Corp. (NYSE: IBM) appear to be in a two-horse race in the European telecom market.

Last year, Spain's Telefónica revealed it was using Microsoft technology to build its Aura digital assistant, which can answer customer queries about usage and even activate smart home services in the Spanish market.

IBM, meanwhile, has landed chatbot and digital assistant deals with UK-based Vodafone and France's Orange. While Vodafone is using IBM's Watson technology for a customer services chatbot, Orange's recently launched banking operation has developed a digital assistant based on Watson technology. IBM has also been working with France's Bouygues in the customer services area, said Philippe Desbiolles, the vice president of IBM's global services business unit in France, during a previous conversation with Light Reading. (See [Robot Wars: Telecom's Looming AI Tussle]( [Chatbot Takes Charge: Vodafone's Customer Services Overhaul]( and [Orange Plans Bank Raid With AI, Digital Weapons](

While the US tech giants have stormed into the market for chatbots and digital assistants, they have not had the same impact on the network operations side of the telco business.

IBM, however, says Watson can also be used for network management and operations. The company has also been involved in a European Commission project known as CogNet, which has looked at using AI to predict traffic demand patterns in future 5G networks. (See [Orange Hails AI Progress in 5G With IBM, Nokia](

Interest in this area could potentially put IBM in competition with traditional network vendors like Huawei Technologies Co. Ltd. and Ericsson AB (Nasdaq: ERIC), both of which are developing AI tools for use in network operations. (See [Humans Beware: Ericsson Readies Machines to Run the Network]( and [Huawei Set for 'Intensive' AI Investment](

— Iain Morris, News Editor, [Light Reading](http://www.lightreading.com/)

[Iain Morris](

International Editor, Light Reading

Iain Morris joined Light Reading as News Editor at the start of 2015 -- and we mean, right at the start. His friends and family were still singing Auld Lang Syne as Iain started sourcing New Year's Eve UK mobile network congestion statistics. Prior to boosting Light Reading's UK-based editorial team numbers (he is based in London, south of the river), Iain was a successful freelance writer and editor who had been covering the telecoms sector for the past 15 years. His work has appeared in publications including The Economist (classy!) and The Observer, besides a variety of trade and business journals. He was previously the lead telecoms analyst for the Economist Intelligence Unit, and before that worked as a features editor at Telecommunications magazine. Iain started out in telecoms as an editor at consulting and market-research company Analysys (now Analysys Mason).

# Anthropic Targets $26 Billion Revenue by 2026 as AI Demand Accelerates

[![Ran Melamed](
Ran MelamedOct 16, 2025, 04:02 AM](

A+

A-

![Anthropic Targets $26 Billion Revenue by 2026 as AI Demand Accelerates](

[Artificial intelligence]( startup Anthropic **[(PC:ANTPQ)]( is [setting ambitious goals]( for the next two years, aiming to nearly triple its annual run rate by 2026. The San Francisco-based company, best known for its Claude language models, expects to reach about $9 billion in revenue by the end of 2025 and as much as $26 billion the following year. If achieved, that pace would place Anthropic among the fastest-growing [AI]( firms serving business clients.

### Elevate Your Investing Strategy:

- Take advantage of [TipRanks Premium at 50% off!]( Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.


At the moment, Anthropic’s annualized revenue is close to $7 billion, a sharp rise from about $1 billion at the start of 2025. This growth has been driven largely by enterprise adoption. Business clients now [account for roughly 80% of total sales]( The company reports that more than 300,000 firms use its Claude tools, while one of its newest products, Claude Code, has already reached nearly $1 billion in annualized revenue since launching earlier this year.

## **Competition with OpenAI Grows**

Anthropic’s rapid climb is bringing it closer to OpenAI **[(PC:OPAIQ)]( the developer of ChatGPT. OpenAI, backed by Microsoft [(MSFT)]( reported an annual run rate of $13 billion in August and said it expects to exceed $20 billion by year-end. While OpenAI continues to lead in the consumer space, Anthropic is strengthening its position in enterprise markets with an emphasis on reliability and data safety.

To build on that momentum, Anthropic recently released [a new model called Claude Haiku 4.5]( It targets cost-focused users by offering performance similar to the mid-tier Claude Sonnet 4 but at one-third of the price and more than twice the speed. This approach is designed to attract large organizations that want scalable AI tools without higher costs.

## **Valuation and Expansion Plans**

After its latest funding round, Anthropic reached [a valuation of about $183 billion]( following a $13 billion Series F led by ICONIQ. The company plans to open its first office in Bengaluru, India, in 2026 and expects to triple its global workforce. It will also expand its applied AI team fivefold to support product development and market expansion.

Anthropic’s rise highlights the [growing demand for enterprise AI solutions]( As more companies seek tools to automate code, summarize information, and improve operations, competition between OpenAI and Anthropic is intensifying. Both firms are working to become the go-to platform for businesses looking for reliable and cost-efficient AI systems.

Using [TipRanks’ Comparison Tool]( we’ve compared some of the notable companies that employ chatbots similar to Claude and ChatGPT. It’s a great way for investors to gain a comprehensive examination of each stock and the AI industry as a whole.

# TIM latest to bring Perplexity to Europe

![stuttgartgermany-02-16-2024personholdingcellphonewithwebpage](

Telecom Italia followed fellow European operator heavyweights [Telefonica]( and Deutsche Telekom in offering consumers free access to advanced generative AI (genAI) platform Perplexity Pro.

With the move, TIM becomes the first operator in Italy to provide its customers with access to the premium AI service. The operator will offer all fixed and mobile customers one year of complimentary access to the service, which typically costs €20 per month.

Perplexity Pro provides real-time, conversational answers supported by verified sources to a wide range of user queries using voice or text input. It enables a range of tasks including content generation, document summarisation, image creation and web page analysis. The platform automatically selects the most suitable large language model (LLM) for each request, drawing on Perplexity’s proprietary model as well as rival LLMs such as ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini.


The offer forms part of TIM’s broader Customer Platform strategy to integrate innovative digital services into its core offering and improve daily user experience through AI.

“This initiative puts artificial intelligence at the service of people, making it truly accessible to all TIM customers,” stated Andrea Rossini, TIM’s Chief Consumer, Small & Medium & Mobile Wholesale Market Officer.

Customers can activate their free Perplexity Pro subscriptions via TIM Party, the operator’s loyalty programme.

TIM’s latest move follows [growing industry interest]( in Perplexity, with Apple linked to a potential acquisition of the AI start-up last month, _Bloomberg_ reported.
